[It] Running the Cluster API E2E tests API Version Upgrade upgrade from an old version of v1beta1 to current, and scale AKS workload clusters created in the old version Should create a management cluster and then upgrade all the providers

ai_summary.summary: The test failed because the 'azureserviceoperator-controller-manager' deployment in the 'capz-system' namespace did not become ready within 5 minutes during the clusterctl upgrade, leading to a timeout when accessing the API server.

ai_analysis.root_cause: The deployment 'azureserviceoperator-controller-manager' in the 'capz-system' namespace failed to reach a ready state. The build-log.txt shows repeated attempts to check the deployment's status, but it never became ready. The API server at 'https://clusterctl-upgrade-management-30t0ki-d9ff9690.canadacentral.cloudapp.azure.com:6443' was unreachable. No manager.log file or deployment/pod YAML artifacts were found in the expected artifact paths, indicating either a failure to create the deployment or missing artifact capture. The root cause could not be determined due to missing logs and manifests.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: No remediation possible from available evidence: artifacts show the deployment was never created (manager.log 404, no YAML files in manifests/deployments directories). Would need to verify deployment creation, image availability, and network policies blocking API server access.

[It] Running the Cluster API E2E tests API Version Upgrade upgrade from the latest version of v1beta1 to current, and scale workload clusters created in the old version Should create a management cluster and then upgrade all the providers

ai_summary.summary: Management Cluster API Server Endpoint Unreachable Due to DNS Resolution Failure

ai_analysis.root_cause: The deployment 'azureserviceoperator-controller-manager' failed readiness checks because the management cluster's API server endpoint (https://clusterctl-upgrade-management-0eevz0-b763168e.canadacentral.cloudapp.azure.com:6443) was unreachable. Build-log.txt shows repeated 'dial tcp: lookup ... no such host' errors (lines 2583-2591) indicating DNS resolution failure. The azure-activity-logs/clusterctl-upgrade-management-0eevz0.log could not be fully read due to truncation, so no conclusive evidence about load balancer degradation timing was found in the available logs.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: No remediation possible from available evidence: (1) Established fact: DNS resolution failed for the management cluster's API server endpoint. (2) Artifacts consulted: build-log.txt (lines 2583-2591). (3) Missing evidence: The azure-activity-logs/clusterctl-upgrade-management-0eevz0.log was truncated during reading, so the exact cause of the DNS resolution failure (e.g., Azure DNS configuration delays, management cluster provisioning issues) remains undetermined.

ai_summary.summary: Test failure due to premature management cluster deletion during cleanup causing deployment readiness check timeout

ai_analysis.root_cause: The test's cleanup process deleted the management cluster before completing deployment readiness checks. Build logs show repeated DNS errors ("no such host") for the management cluster's API server endpoint (clusterctl-upgrade-management-m7v3cr-a79fc73a.switzerlandnorth.cloudapp.azure.com) during deployment status verification. Required artifacts for the 'cluster-cleanup-stuck' recipe (stuck resource YAML and capz-controller-manager logs) were unavailable due to cluster deletion, preventing confirmation of Azure resource finalizer blocks.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Modify test logic to ensure deployment readiness checks complete before initiating management cluster deletion. Add explicit waits or reorder cleanup steps in test/e2e/e2e_suite_test.go to prevent premature cluster deletion. For example, add a wait condition after deployment verification before triggering cluster deletion.
